strategy_md:
# Baseline Prompt Only for The Enron Investigation Brief

This weak baseline uses a tiny sample and conservative prompt-only synthesis. It is reproducible as a fixture, not competitive. It intentionally documents missing evidence, sample bias, and overinterpretation risk.

participant.description: Weak baseline using a small sample and direct prompting.

answer.title: Sample Brazil Public Money Trail — Health Policy Signals, Baseline Prompt Only
answer.executive_summary: This weak baseline demonstrates how the arena renders a civic public-money trail without making accusations. It uses a tiny sample and clearly separates verified public-record facts from hypotheses. It is not a gold answer and should not be used to infer wrongdoing.

sections.title: Policy Theme and Scope
items: Sources: Câmara dos Deputados sample records and a clearly marked transparency-like local sample if no API token is available.

sections.title: Direct Links vs Hypotheses
items: Direct documented links identify records inside a source system.
Same-theme links show analytical proximity only.
No causal claim is made between legislative action and public spending.

claims.claim: The selected health theme can be represented in legislative records and public-spending-like records, but the sample does not establish a causal path between them.
A useful money-trail page should classify links as direct documented links, same-theme links, same-actor links, same-agency links, same-time-window links, or weak/hypothetical links.
claims.notes: Preparation pipeline produces candidate link classifications.

claims.claim: The baseline prompt-only approach is inadequate for serious civic accountability because it does not perform robust entity resolution, API coverage checks, or independent verification.

evidence.title: Câmara dos Deputados Dados Abertos
evidence.excerpt: Official public API source for propositions, deputies, votes and legislative events.

evidence.title: Portal da Transparência API de Dados
evidence.excerpt: Official public API source for federal transparency data; real API use requires a token.

evidence.title: Link classification methodology
evidence.excerpt: Candidate links must be classified so readers can tell direct documentation from same-theme or weak analytical proximity.

evidence.title: Baseline strategy note
evidence.excerpt: The weak baseline uses a tiny sample and conservative prompt-only synthesis. It documents missing evidence and avoids accusations.

timeline.title: Policy theme selected
timeline.description: Health is used as the default sample policy theme for local mode.

timeline.title: Transparency token check
timeline.description: If TRANSPARENCIA_API_TOKEN is unavailable, the task writes mock transparency rows for UI testing only.

entities.name: Portal da Transparência
entities.description: Official federal transparency API source; token required for real data calls.

entities.name: Health policy theme
entities.description: Default sample theme used to trace policy-to-money signals.

risks.title: Correlation mistaken for causation
risks.description: Same-theme or same-time-window records do not prove that a deputy, bill, agency, or supplier caused a spending outcome.

risks.title: Mock sample misread as official spending data
risks.description: When no Portal da Transparência token is available, generated transparency rows are local fixtures only.

uncertainties.description: The baseline does not verify a full policy-to-spending chain.
uncertainties.why_it_matters: Readers need to know which links are documented and which are merely analytical hypotheses.
uncertainties.missing_evidence: Full API run, entity resolution, source-specific spending endpoints, and human review.

recommendations.title: Run deterministic public-data joins before LLM synthesis
recommendations.description: Join records by theme, agency, actor, time window, and documented identifiers before asking an LLM to write conclusions.
recommendations.rationale: Reduces hallucination and makes civic claims inspectable.

recommendations.title: Add human investigation questions
recommendations.description: End with questions for journalists, auditors, researchers, or citizens rather than unsupported accusations.
recommendations.rationale: Keeps the task useful and safe for public accountability.

limitations: Placeholder/sample content only; not a gold answer.
No accusation of wrongdoing is made or implied.
Mock transparency rows, if used, are local demo data and not official records.
The baseline does not perform robust entity resolution, deduplication, or full API coverage analysis.

context_trace.strategy_summary: A weak baseline that reads task metadata and a tiny sample, writes conservative claims, and explicitly avoids causal or corruption claims.

what_was_ignored.description: Full Portal da Transparência API coverage

known_failure_modes: Does not resolve entities across sources.
May rely on mock sample data when token is missing.

score.notes: Weak baseline. It is safe and transparent, but it does not perform real multi-source joining or verification.

strategy_md:
# Baseline Prompt Only for Brazil Public Money Trail

This weak baseline is designed to make the task visible in the arena, not to be competitive.

It uses a tiny sample and task metadata to produce a conservative civic brief. It explicitly avoids claims of corruption, influence, or causality. The baseline is useful because it shows the expected structure: policy scope, legislative and spending timelines, actor/institution mapping, claim/evidence tables, direct-vs-hypothetical link framing, and limitations.

## What it tried

- Identify official data sources.
- State a default theme: health.
- Explain how legislative records and spending records could be connected.
- Mark missing evidence and unresolved questions.

## What it did not do

- It did not run robust entity resolution.
- It did not perform deterministic joins across all official APIs.
- It did not verify contracts, beneficiaries, or fiscalization data.
- It did not prove a causal path from a bill to a spending record.

## Why this baseline is weak

Public accountability requires careful joins, source-specific coverage checks, and human review. A prompt-only strategy can write a readable summary, but it cannot safely establish a policy-to-money trail without structured data preparation.

participant.description: Weak baseline using a small sample and direct prompting.
